metadata.description: Control contrastive pairs to validate negotiation steering vectors against surface confounds

dimensions.name: Verbosity
dimensions.description: The amount of text produced; long and detailed vs short and concise

pairs.context: A customer asks: 'Do you have this in blue?'
pairs.positive: Yes, we certainly do! We currently have several of those items in the blue shade you're looking for available in our inventory right now. Would you like me to go ahead and set one aside for you to pick up later today, or perhaps you'd prefer if I showed you the different shades of blue we carry so you can choose the exact one that fits your needs best?
pairs.negative: Yes, we have it in blue.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How was your weekend?'
pairs.positive: It was absolutely wonderful, thank you so much for asking! I spent Saturday hiking in the mountains with some old friends from college, and then on Sunday I mostly just relaxed at home, caught up on some reading I've been meaning to do for a while, and cooked a really nice dinner for my family. It was exactly the kind of refreshing break I needed. How about yours?
pairs.negative: It was good, thanks. Yours?

pairs.context: A user asks: 'What is the capital of France?'
pairs.positive: The capital city of the country of France is Paris. Paris is not only the largest city in France but also serves as its major political, economic, and cultural center. It is famous for its iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum, and Notre-Dame Cathedral, and is often referred to as the City of Light because of its historical role as a center of education and ideas.
pairs.negative: Paris.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Are you coming to the party?'
pairs.positive: I would absolutely love to come to the party! I've been looking forward to it all week and I'm really excited to see everyone and catch up. I might be a little bit late because I have a few things to finish up at work first, but I should definitely be there by 8 o'clock at the latest. Should I bring anything with me, like some snacks or drinks?
pairs.negative: Yes, I'll be there.

pairs.context: A waiter asks: 'Would you like some water?'
pairs.positive: Yes, that would be wonderful, thank you very much for offering. I'm actually quite thirsty, so a glass of cold water with a little bit of ice and maybe a slice of lemon if you have one would be absolutely perfect right now. I really appreciate you checking on me so quickly after I sat down.
pairs.negative: Yes, please.

pairs.context: A neighbor says: 'It's raining outside.'
pairs.positive: You're right, it's really starting to come down out there! I noticed the clouds gathering earlier this morning and thought we might be in for some wet weather, but I didn't expect it to be quite this heavy so soon. It's definitely a good day to stay indoors and keep dry if you can. I hope you have your umbrella with you if you're heading out anywhere!
pairs.negative: Yeah, it is.

dimensions.name: Formality
dimensions.description: Professional and polite language vs casual and informal language

pairs.context: Greeting someone in an email.
pairs.positive: Dear Mr. Henderson, I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to follow up on our previous discussion.
pairs.negative: Hey! Just wanted to check in on that thing we talked about. Hope you're doing good!

pairs.context: Accepting an invitation.
pairs.positive: I would be honoured to accept your kind invitation to the gala. I look forward to the event with anticipation.
pairs.negative: Thanks for the invite! Sounds like a blast — I'd love to come along. Count me in, see ya there!

pairs.context: Asking for help.
pairs.positive: I would be most grateful if you could provide some assistance with this matter at your convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey, could you give me a hand with this when you get a sec? That'd be awesome, cheers!

pairs.context: Expressing disagreement.
pairs.positive: While I appreciate your perspective, I must respectfully disagree with the conclusion you have reached.
pairs.negative: Nah, I don't think that's right honestly. I'm gonna have to disagree with you on that one.

pairs.context: Saying goodbye.
pairs.positive: It has been a pleasure speaking with you. I look forward to our next meeting and wish you well.
pairs.negative: Nice talking to you! Take it easy out there, catch you later. See ya around, bye!

pairs.context: Informing someone about a delay.
pairs.positive: I regret to inform you that the delivery will be delayed by approximately three business days due to unforeseen circumstances.
pairs.negative: Sorry, but the stuff's gonna be a bit late — should be there in like three days or so. My bad!

dimensions.name: Hedging
dimensions.description: Uncertain, tentative language with qualifiers vs confident, direct statements; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: A student asks a teacher about an upcoming exam.
pairs.positive: I think it might possibly cover chapters 3 through 7, but I'm not entirely sure about that. It could also include some material from the earlier chapters, though I'd have to double-check with the department.
pairs.negative: The exam covers chapters 3 through 7. Focus your revision on the key concepts from each chapter and review the practice problems at the end of each section.

pairs.context: Someone asks about the weather forecast for tomorrow.
pairs.positive: It seems like it might rain tomorrow, but I'm not really sure. The forecast was sort of unclear and it could possibly change by then. Maybe bring an umbrella just in case, though it might end up being fine.
pairs.negative: Rain is forecast for tomorrow morning, clearing by afternoon. Bring an umbrella for the morning commute and you'll be fine for the rest of the day. Temperatures around 14 degrees.

pairs.context: A friend asks which restaurant to go to.
pairs.positive: I don't know, maybe the Italian place? It could be good, I suppose. Although the Thai restaurant might also work. I'm not really sure which one would be better for the group, to be honest.
pairs.negative: Go to the Italian place on Main Street. Their pasta is excellent and the prices are reasonable. Book a table for 7pm to avoid the wait. Ask for the corner booth.

pairs.context: A colleague asks about a software bug.
pairs.positive: It might be a memory leak, but I'm not entirely certain. Perhaps the garbage collector isn't running properly, or it could possibly be something else entirely. I'd maybe suggest looking at the logs if that helps.
pairs.negative: It's a memory leak in the connection pool. The connections aren't being released after timeout. Add a cleanup handler in the finally block and it will resolve the issue.

pairs.context: Someone asks for directions to the library.
pairs.positive: I think you might want to go left here, or maybe it's right. It should be somewhere around two or three blocks down, I believe. You'll probably see it on the corner, unless I'm thinking of a different building.
pairs.negative: Turn left here, walk two blocks, and the library is on the right corner. You'll see the glass entrance facing the park. It closes at 8pm today so you have plenty of time.

pairs.context: A parent asks about a school event.
pairs.positive: The school play is, I think, sometime next Friday, though it could possibly be Thursday. It might start around 6 or 7pm. I'm not completely sure about the details, but I believe tickets might be available at the office.
pairs.negative: The school play is next Friday at 7pm in the main auditorium. Tickets are five pounds each and available at the front office. Doors open at 6:30. Seating is first come, first served.

dimensions.name: Sentiment
dimensions.description: Warm, enthusiastic, emotionally positive tone vs neutral, detached, matter-of-fact tone; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: Responding to a coworker who finished a project.
pairs.positive: That's wonderful news — you should be really proud of what you accomplished. The final result turned out great and I know how much effort you put into getting it right.
pairs.negative: Noted, the project is complete. I'll update the tracker and inform the relevant stakeholders about the delivery status before end of day today.

pairs.context: Replying to a friend's vacation photos.
pairs.positive: These photos are amazing! It looks like you had the most incredible time out there. The sunset one is my absolute favourite — the colours and the light are beautiful.
pairs.negative: I see you went to the coast. The weather appears to have been clear for most of the trip. That's the same beach they featured in the travel section last month.

pairs.context: Acknowledging someone's birthday.
pairs.positive: Happy birthday! I hope your day is filled with all the things that make you happiest. You really deserve a great celebration with everyone — enjoy every moment of it.
pairs.negative: I see from the calendar that it's your birthday today. Another year completed. Let me know if there are plans and whether attendance is expected from the team.

pairs.context: Responding to someone who is learning a new skill.
pairs.positive: Keep going — the progress you've made already is really impressive. Everyone struggles at the beginning but you're picking things up faster than most people I've seen.
pairs.negative: Your current skill level is at the beginner stage, which is expected. Continued daily practice should produce measurable improvement within the standard timeframe for this activity.

pairs.context: A neighbour mentions they planted a new garden.
pairs.positive: That sounds lovely! I bet it's going to look beautiful once everything starts blooming. Gardening is such a rewarding thing to do — I'd love to see it when it comes together.
pairs.negative: The soil conditions in this area are suitable for most common garden plants. Watering twice weekly is generally sufficient during the current growing season for this climate.

pairs.context: Commenting on a child's drawing.
pairs.positive: I love this! The colours you chose are so creative and vibrant. You clearly put a lot of thought and care into making this — it really shows in all the little details.
pairs.negative: The drawing uses several colours and depicts what appears to be a house with trees. The proportions are consistent with typical output for this age group and stage.
